questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall the order of electromagnetic waves
questions.question: Which electromagnetic wave has the highest frequency?
questions.correctAnswer: Gamma rays
questions.explanation: Gamma rays have the highest frequency and shortest wavelength in the electromagnetic spectrum.
questions.senNote: Gamma rays have the highest frequency and shortest wavelength in the electromagnetic spectrum.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall the order of electromagnetic waves
questions.question: On a clinic worksheet, a learner checks: a student orders the EM spectrum from low to high frequency. Which is at the high end?
questions.correctAnswer: Gamma rays
questions.explanation: Gamma rays are at the highest-frequency end of the electromagnetic spectrum.
questions.senNote: Gamma rays are at the highest-frequency end of the electromagnetic spectrum.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall the order of electromagnetic waves
questions.question: On a museum label, a visitor reads: a results table lists radio, infrared, ultraviolet and gamma. Which has the highest frequency?
questions.correctAnswer: Gamma rays
questions.explanation: Gamma rays are at the highest-frequency end of the electromagnetic spectrum.
questions.senNote: Gamma rays are at the highest-frequency end of the electromagnetic spectrum.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe refraction into a denser medium
questions.question: Light passes from air into glass. Which change happens to the light?
options: It speeds up and bends away from the normal.
It keeps the same speed and does not change direction.
It slows down and bends away from the normal.
It slows down and bends towards the normal.
questions.correctAnswer: It slows down and bends towards the normal.
questions.explanation: Light slows down in glass, so it bends towards the normal.
questions.senNote: Light slows down in glass, so it bends towards the normal.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe refraction into a denser medium
questions.question: On a clinic worksheet, a learner checks: light crosses from air into a glass block. What happens to its speed and path?
options: It slows down and bends towards the normal.
questions.correctAnswer: It slows down and bends towards the normal.
questions.explanation: When light enters glass from air, it slows and bends towards the normal.
questions.senNote: When light enters glass from air, it slows and bends towards the normal.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe refraction into a denser medium
questions.question: On a museum label, a visitor reads: a results table shows a ray bending towards the normal as it enters glass. Why?
options: It slows down and bends towards the normal.
questions.correctAnswer: It slows down and bends towards the normal.
questions.explanation: When light enters glass from air, it slows and bends towards the normal.
questions.senNote: When light enters glass from air, it slows and bends towards the normal.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe refraction into a denser medium
questions.question: On a school lab card, a pupil answers: what happens when light passes from air into a denser transparent material?
options: It slows down and bends towards the normal.
It speeds up and bends away.
questions.correctAnswer: It slows down and bends towards the normal.
questions.explanation: When light enters glass from air, it slows and bends towards the normal.
questions.senNote: When light enters glass from air, it slows and bends towards the normal.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe refraction into a denser medium

options: It slows down and bends towards the normal.
questions.correctAnswer: It slows down and bends towards the normal.
questions.explanation: When light enters glass from air, it slows and bends towards the normal.
questions.senNote: When light enters glass from air, it slows and bends towards the normal.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Link frequency to pitch
questions.question: Which change makes a sound higher in pitch?
options: Increasing the frequency of the wave
questions.correctAnswer: Increasing the frequency of the wave
questions.explanation: Pitch depends on frequency. Higher frequency means higher pitch.
questions.senNote: Pitch depends on frequency. Higher frequency means higher pitch.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Link frequency to pitch
questions.question: On a clinic worksheet, a learner checks: a student tightens a string and the note is higher. What changed?
options: Increasing the frequency of the wave
Change the wave from longitudinal to transverse.
questions.correctAnswer: Increasing the frequency of the wave
questions.explanation: A higher pitch means the sound frequency is higher.
questions.senNote: A higher pitch means the sound frequency is higher.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Link frequency to pitch
questions.question: On a museum label, a visitor reads: a results table compares two sound waves. Which change gives a higher pitch?
options: Increasing the frequency of the wave
Remove the particles from the medium.
questions.correctAnswer: Increasing the frequency of the wave
questions.explanation: A higher pitch means the sound frequency is higher.
questions.senNote: A higher pitch means the sound frequency is higher.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Link frequency to pitch
questions.question: On a school lab card, a pupil answers: what must increase for a sound wave to be heard as a higher note?
options: Increasing the frequency of the wave
Lower the energy carried by the wave.
questions.correctAnswer: Increasing the frequency of the wave
questions.explanation: A higher pitch means the sound frequency is higher.
questions.senNote: A higher pitch means the sound frequency is higher.

questions.skill: Link frequency to pitch

options: Increasing the frequency of the wave

questions.explanation: A higher pitch means the sound frequency is higher.
questions.senNote: A higher pitch means the sound frequency is higher.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain one use of ultrasound
questions.question: Why is ultrasound useful for scanning inside the human body?
options: It can be reflected to make images and it is non-ionising.
questions.correctAnswer: It can be reflected to make images and it is non-ionising.
questions.explanation: Ultrasound reflects at boundaries between tissues and is safe because it is non-ionising.
questions.senNote: Ultrasound reflects at boundaries between tissues and is safe because it is non-ionising.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain one use of ultrasound
questions.question: On a clinic worksheet, a learner checks: a student explains a pregnancy scan. Why can ultrasound be used instead of ionising radiation?
options: It can be reflected to make images and it is non-ionising.
questions.correctAnswer: It can be reflected to make images and it is non-ionising.
questions.explanation: Ultrasound reflects at boundaries to form images and is non-ionising.
questions.senNote: Ultrasound reflects at boundaries to form images and is non-ionising.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain one use of ultrasound
questions.question: On a museum label, a visitor reads: a results table shows echoes from boundaries inside the body. What makes ultrasound useful?
options: It can be reflected to make images and it is non-ionising.
questions.correctAnswer: It can be reflected to make images and it is non-ionising.
questions.explanation: Ultrasound reflects at boundaries to form images and is non-ionising.
questions.senNote: Ultrasound reflects at boundaries to form images and is non-ionising.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain one use of ultrasound
questions.question: On a school lab card, a pupil answers: why can ultrasound make medical images without using ionising radiation?
options: It can be reflected to make images and it is non-ionising.
It can only travel through a vacuum.
questions.correctAnswer: It can be reflected to make images and it is non-ionising.
questions.explanation: Ultrasound reflects at boundaries to form images and is non-ionising.
questions.senNote: Ultrasound reflects at boundaries to form images and is non-ionising.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain one use of ultrasound
questions.question: On a factory safety note, an apprentice reviews: a scanner sends high-frequency sound into the body. How can this produce an image?
options: It is below the range of human hearing.
It can be reflected to make images and it is non-ionising.
questions.correctAnswer: It can be reflected to make images and it is non-ionising.
questions.explanation: Ultrasound reflects at boundaries to form images and is non-ionising.
questions.senNote: Ultrasound reflects at boundaries to form images and is non-ionising.

questions.explanation: Ultrasound reflects at boundaries to form images and is non-ionising.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Use wave speed = frequency x wavelength
questions.question: A wave has a frequency of 15 Hz and a wavelength of 0.6 m. What is the wave speed?
questions.correctAnswer: 9 m/s
